headline: Updated Guidance Refines LDL-C Targets in Very-High-Risk Patients
summary: New consensus guidance lowers the LDL-C target for patients with established cardiovascular disease and adds a stepwise approach to combination therapy when statins alone are insufficient.
expanded: The revised guidance reflects five years of additional outcomes data from combination lipid-lowering trials. It applies to adults with confirmed atherosclerotic cardiovascular disease and introduces a structured escalation pathway.
keyFindings: New target: LDL-C below 55 mg/dL for very-high-risk patients
Combination therapy recommended earlier in the treatment pathway
clinicalRelevance: May prompt earlier initiation of combination therapy rather than sequential statin titration alone.
limitation: Guidance based on pooled trial data; individual patient risk should still guide therapy.

headline: Five Simple Habits That Lower Diabetes Risk
summary: A large cohort study confirms that a handful of everyday habits meaningfully reduce the risk of developing Type 2 diabetes, even in people with a family history.
expanded: Researchers followed over 90,000 adults for a decade, tracking diet, activity, sleep, and weight trends. Participants who maintained several protective habits together saw the greatest risk reduction.
keyFindings: 30+ minutes of daily activity linked to lower risk
Consistent sleep timing was independently protective
Combined habits outperformed any single change
clinicalRelevance: Useful talking points for preventive counselling in primary care visits.
limitation: Observational cohort study; cannot fully separate habit effects from other lifestyle factors.
sourceTitle: Long-Term Lifestyle Patterns and Diabetes Incidence

headline: WHO Updates Vaccination Schedule for Adolescents
summary: Revised recommendations extend catch-up eligibility and clarify spacing between adolescent booster doses.
expanded: The update responds to shifting regional coverage gaps identified in recent surveillance data, with clarified guidance for clinics managing mixed-schedule patients.
clinicalRelevance: Clinics should review adolescent catch-up protocols against the new spacing rules.
limitation: Regional health authorities may adapt timelines differently.
